scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every practical GET list/detail resource and every dialect-expressible mutation documented under the Webflow Data API v2 reference (verified against the official /data/v2.0.0/llms.txt endpoint index and each endpoint's own OpenAPI fragment) is now covered_by a stream or write action for a single configured site. Endpoints requiring an OAuth Data-Client app token (custom code registration) rather than a plain Site/OAuth access token, Enterprise-only endpoints (workspace management, audit logs, site activity logs, 301-redirect/robots.txt/well-known-file site configuration), binary/multipart upload flows, and page/component DOM-node content (no practical flat-record schema) are excluded with a specific reason each.

excluded.reason: identity/introspection endpoint describing the authorizing user of the current token, not a syncable data object
token metadata/scopes introspection, not a syncable data object
single-site detail is a strict subset of the fields already returned by GET /v2/sites (list); the sites stream covers every site the token can access, including the configured one

publishes the entire site (or a single page) live to one or more domains, rate-limited to 1 successful publish per minute; a whole-site deploy action is an administrative/build-trigger operation, not a per-record data mutation the write dialect's per-record body/path model represents well
single-page metadata detail is a strict subset of the fields already returned by the pages stream's list endpoint
updates page SEO/Open Graph settings; a legitimate write candidate but not yet modeled this pass to keep page-settings and page-DOM mutation scope together for a future increment

ENGINE_GAP: returns a deeply recursive DOM node tree (text/component-instance nodes with nested children of heterogeneous node types) with no fixed flat-record shape; no declarative schema in this dialect can represent an arbitrarily-nested, type-polymorphic node tree as draft-07 properties without either flattening (losing structure) or a passthrough blob (defeats the purpose of a typed stream)

ENGINE_GAP: the write body is an array of arbitrarily-typed/nested DOM node update objects (text vs. component-instance-property nodes have different shapes) keyed by node id; the write dialect's flat body/path_fields/body_fields model has no way to express a heterogeneous nested-node-array payload
same heterogeneous nested-node-tree shape as page DOM content; no flat declarative schema represents it
same heterogeneous nested-node-array write payload as page DOM update; not dialect-expressible
component property default values are a nested detail view of a single components stream record, not an independently syncable collection
updates localized default property values for a component; a narrow, rarely-needed write not modeled this pass
single-collection detail (schema/fields) is a strict subset of what the collections stream's list response already returns per collection
creates a brand-new CMS collection (schema definition), a structural/schema-design operation rather than a data-record mutation; out of scope for this pass, which targets record-level writes
permanently deletes an entire CMS collection and all its items/schema; a structural admin action, far higher blast radius than any per-item write modeled here
adds a new field (schema change) to a collection; structural schema-design operation, not a data-record write
modifies a collection field's schema definition; structural, not a data-record write
permanently removes a field (and its data across every item in the collection) from a CMS schema; structural admin action
single-item detail is a strict subset of the fields already returned by the collection_items stream's list endpoint

the published/live variant of the same items already read by the collection_items stream (staged); reading both staged and live copies of every item would double the sync volume for the same underlying content with no new fields
single-item live detail; same reasoning as the live list endpoint above
creates an item directly in the live (published) copy, bypassing the staged review step; publish_collection_item already covers the sanctioned staged-then-publish write path
updates the live copy directly, bypassing staged review; update_collection_item + publish_collection_item already cover the sanctioned two-step write path
unpublishes items from the live site directly; a distinct action from delete_collection_item (which removes the staged copy) not modeled this pass
single-form schema detail is a strict subset of the fields already returned by the forms stream's list endpoint
per-form submission listing is a filtered subset of the site-wide form_submissions stream (which already supports narrowing via elementId); reading both would duplicate every submission record
single-submission detail is a strict subset of the fields already returned by the form_submissions stream

permanently and irrecoverably deletes a customer/visitor-submitted form response; higher-risk than the modeled hidden-field update and not requested by any known reverse-ETL use case for this connector

Register/List Script endpoints require a bearer token from an OAuth Data-Client App code-grant flow specifically (per the endpoint's own doc note), not the Site-token/plain-OAuth-access-token auth this bundle's spec.json models
same Data-Client-App-only auth requirement as the custom-code list endpoint
same Data-Client-App-only auth requirement as the custom-code list endpoint
same Data-Client-App-only auth requirement as the custom-code list endpoint
same Data-Client-App-only auth requirement as the custom-code list endpoint
same Data-Client-App-only auth requirement as the custom-code list endpoint
same Data-Client-App-only auth requirement as the custom-code list endpoint
same Data-Client-App-only auth requirement as the custom-code list endpoint
same Data-Client-App-only auth requirement as the custom-code list endpoint
same Data-Client-App-only auth requirement as the custom-code list endpoint
single-asset detail is a strict subset of the fields already returned by the assets stream's list endpoint

the first step of a two-phase upload flow: this call only returns a presigned uploadUrl/uploadDetails; the actual file bytes are then POSTed directly to Amazon S3, outside this API entirely and outside the write dialect's single-request JSON/form body model
single-folder detail is a strict subset of the fields already returned by the asset_folders stream's list endpoint
creates a new (empty) asset folder; a low-value structural write not modeled this pass
custom font management is a design-asset concern with no read/write use case identified for a data-integration connector this pass; deferred alongside custom-code
single-font detail is a subset of the (unmodeled) custom-fonts list above
font upload is a multipart font-file upload flow, not a JSON/form body
font metadata update; deferred alongside the rest of custom-fonts support
replaces the font file itself via multipart upload
deferred alongside the rest of custom-fonts support
deferred alongside the rest of custom-fonts support
site-collaboration Comments feature (Designer in-app annotation threads); not application/business data, no reverse-ETL use case identified
single-thread detail is a subset of the (unmodeled) comment-threads list above
same Comments feature scope narrowing as the comment-threads list
Webflow Analyze API is a separate, additionally-gated analytics product tier from the base Data API; not confirmed available under this connector's plain site/OAuth token
same Analyze-API gating as the traffic report endpoint
same Analyze-API gating as the traffic report endpoint
same Analyze-API gating as the traffic report endpoint
same Analyze-API gating as the traffic report endpoint
single-product-and-SKUs detail is a strict subset of the fields already returned by the products stream's list endpoint

creates a brand-new Product+SKU pair; deferred to keep this pass's ecommerce writes focused on operational updates (order fulfillment/inventory) over catalog authoring, which typically happens in the Webflow Designer/E-commerce panel, not via reverse ETL
updates product-level fields (name/description/publish status); deferred alongside product creation for the same catalog-authoring reasoning
creates additional SKU variants for an existing product; catalog-authoring, deferred alongside product create/update
updates SKU-level fields (price/name); catalog-authoring, deferred alongside product create/update

single-SKU inventory read has no independent list form and is most naturally consumed alongside update_inventory's own read-modify-write workflow rather than as a separate sync stream; also would require an ENGINE_GAP fan-out keyed off each product's nested SKU id array, which this dialect's fan_out (top-level records_path only) cannot express
single-order detail is a strict subset of the fields already returned by the orders stream's list endpoint

reverses a fulfillment; the inverse of fulfill_order, not modeled this pass to keep the write surface's operational footprint minimal (unfulfilling a real shipped order is a rare correction, not a typical reverse-ETL action)
single-webhook detail is a strict subset of the fields already returned by the webhooks stream's list endpoint
site-configuration integration toggle (Google Tag Manager container id), not application/business data; no reverse-ETL use case identified
same Google Tag Manager site-configuration scope narrowing as the read above
same Google Tag Manager site-configuration scope narrowing
same Google Tag Manager site-configuration scope narrowing
Enterprise-plan-only Workspace Audit Logs API; not available for a base Site/OAuth-token-authenticated site as this bundle's spec.json models
Enterprise-plan-only Site Activity Logs API
Enterprise-only 301-redirect site-configuration surface (distinct from the base redirects stream already covered); requires an Enterprise-plan workspace
same Enterprise-only 301-redirect site-configuration gating
same Enterprise-only 301-redirect site-configuration gating
same Enterprise-only 301-redirect site-configuration gating
Enterprise-only site-configuration surface
Enterprise-only site-configuration surface
Enterprise-only site-configuration surface
Enterprise-only site-configuration surface
Enterprise-only site-configuration surface
Enterprise-only site-configuration surface
Enterprise-only Workspace Management API (programmatic site create/update/delete/plan lookup), not available for a base Site/OAuth-token-authenticated single-site connector as this bundle models
Enterprise-only Workspace Management site update
Enterprise-only Workspace Management site deletion; also destructively removes an entire site
Enterprise-only Workspace Management API
